answer:
\boxed{A}

An integer overflow occurs when an arithmetic operation exceeds the maximum (or minimum) value a fixed-size integer can hold, causing the result to "wrap around" to a smaller or negative value due to modulo arithmetic. This is a common issue in low-level programming (e.g., C/C++) where integers have fixed bit-widths. The other options describe unrelated concepts like pointer misuse (B), buffer overflow (C), or memory exhaustion (D).
